**Planning deep inspection of project_link rule and logs**

I'm focusing on reading the full run block in the Snakefile around project_link (lines 850-1030) to analyze how the share URL is parsed and matched with the log’s empty response error; simultaneously, I’ll read the entire relevant log file to understand the actual API response and failure context.**Explaining project_link failure**

- The log at [logs/project_link_lane7_R1-26_I1-10_I2-10_R2-90_WagaL_10x5V2_B3_B4_HAT3_HAT4_BCR_4plex.log](logs/project_link_lane7_R1-26_I1-10_I2-10_R2-90_WagaL_10x5V2_B3_B4_HAT3_HAT4_BCR_4plex.log) shows the Nextcloud POST request returned an empty response body after "Response:", so the regex failed to extract the expected `<url>`.
- The relevant code at [Snakefile](Snakefile#L879-L993) attempts to create the share link by parsing `<url>` from the response; an empty body leads to logging "Could not extract URL" and silently succeeding without creating a link.
- Likely reasons include authentication failure, network issues, or permission problems causing Nextcloud to respond without the expected data; though the code now logs return codes and output length, the log suggests this run predates those enhancements.
- To debug, re-run the curl POST manually with verbose output (`curl -v`) to check authentication and connectivity, verify credentials have not changed, confirm the Nextcloud path is correct and accessible (including trailing slashes), and watch for server-side errors or rate limiting.
- The rule will succeed without the link due to its design; resolving the API response issue is needed to restore link creation.- The log for this project (`project_link_lane7_R1-26_I1-10_I2-10_R2-90_WagaL_10x5V2_B3_B4_HAT3_HAT4_BCR_4plex.log`) shows the curl POST to the Nextcloud sharing API returned an empty body (“Response:” with nothing after it), so the parser couldn’t find a `<url>` tag and wrote “Could not extract URL from response.”
- In the rule logic (`undefined`), the share link is only recorded when `<url>…</url>` exists in the response; an empty or error response leaves `share_url` unset and the rule finishes without creating an entry.
- Likely causes: Nextcloud rejected the request (auth/password change, permissions) or the server returned an error with no body. Rerun the curl with `-v` or check the return code/stderr (the current code logs these on rerun) and confirm the credentials and path `/DragenExt/xR078_workflow/output/lane7_R1-26_I1-10_I2-10_R2-90/WagaL_10x5V2_B3_B4_HAT3_HAT4_BCR_4plex` exist and are shareable.
